description: Eval set for the v2 startup financial modeling skill: a 12-sheet integrated architecture (Guide, Summary, Assumptions, Revenue Build, Cost Build, People Plan, P&L, BS, CF, Financing, Cap Table, Evidence) built on an economic kernel with a hybrid monthly x annual period axis, lean closed mode bundles, scenario toggle mechanism, evidence registers, and machine-audited xlsx quality gates.

evals.name: full_hybrid_jp_fundraising
evals.prompt: 国内SaaSスタートアップのシリーズA調達に向けて、5年の統合財務モデルをxlsxで作って。月次の資金繰りが見たい。月額12万円のサブスク、顧客120→3,400社、調達4億円、法定福利費率15%、期首現金1.2億円。
evals.expected_output: Full-mode v2 workbook (12 sheets: Guide, Summary, Assumptions, Revenue Build, Cost Build, People Plan, P&L, BS, CF, Financing, Cap Table, Evidence) on the hybrid axis: a 24-column monthly window (labels YYYY/MM) anchored to the fiscal year in progress, followed by annual FY columns, months ruler in row 5, freeze at F7, one master check on Summary echoed on every period sheet.

assertions.text: Workbook contains exactly the 12-sheet full bundle (Guide, Summary, Assumptions, Revenue Build, Cost Build, People Plan, P&L, BS, CF, Financing, Cap Table, Evidence) — no legacy sheets (Kernel, Driver Tree, Capital Stack, KPI, Scenarios, Sensitivity, Benchmarks, Exit Waterfall)

Period-axis sheets follow the v2 header contract: title row 2 with master-check echo, unit caption + FY ruler row 4, integer months ruler row 5, period headers row 6, data from row 8, freeze pane at the first period column row 7, uniform 11.5 period-column width

Hybrid axis renders 24 monthly columns labeled YYYY/MM starting at the first month of the fiscal year in progress (never a fully-past first fiscal year), then one annual column per remaining FY, with the monthly-to-annual boundary declared by a medium rule
Every per-period engine formula reads the months ruler (F$5) so one row formula is copy-identical across monthly and annual columns (R1C1-normalized R17)

JP practice is carried in the statements: tax-exclusive P&L with NOL-aware tax and half-year-convention D&A note, tax-inclusive CF with 消費税/源泉・社保 balance rows and a stated simplification note, AR/AP driven by 回収・支払サイト when stated
Statutory welfare rate scales base salary into fully-loaded comp on People Plan and the scaling is labeled
P&L, BS, CF, Cap Table, and Summary each carry at least one numeric check row (0 = OK, OK/ERROR format); Summary consolidates every registered check into a master check
Financing shows Sources & Uses with a cumulative-consistency check, post-raise runway floor check (>= 18 months), and a live downside funding gap

Any xlsx output follows the canonical workbook design tokens: Arial 10pt default/body font, semantic font colors, role-based alignment, Google-Sheets-safe column widths, compact row heights, no wrap/freeze/merge, no unnecessary wrapping on horizontal-read titles/instructions/notes/bullets/source caveats when blank unmerged unstyled overflow cells are available, exact row heights for approved bounded table prose exceptions, rectangular semantic fill and border spans including member blank cells, table-width row rules that start after hierarchy spacer columns, no per-row gridline borders, borderless memo/source/note cells, three semantic border styles, black border colors by default, no rainbow or decorative background fills, role-based sparse fill palette, no adjacent repeated prominent border rows, sparse non-consecutive accents, clean print/canvas bounds, and raw-value number formats, and sheet-specific quality gates for purpose, source boundary, dependency flow, checks, and interpretation, and IB-style text positioning with left-aligned labels/sources/notes, right-aligned values/formulas/units, and centered period/scenario/matrix headers only, and IB-style font-size discipline with a 9/10/11/14pt generated cell palette and no 8pt squeezed cell text or 16pt+ presentation headings.

Every included sheet has a sheet-specific purpose, traceable source/evidence boundary, necessary formulas or checks, explicit dependency logic to upstream sheets, and an output interpretation surface where the sheet informs a decision. Focused tasks include only sheets needed for the decision and required dependencies; they do not add full-workbook tabs merely because the template supports them.

Workbook text wrapping follows IB model discipline: generated cells keep wrap_text off by default; horizontal-read titles, instructions, notes, bullets, source caveats, and memo lines read through blank unmerged unstyled overflow cells; horizontal-read text is not trapped at the final print/render column or blocked by styled overflow cells; wrapping or manual line breaks appear only for user-approved bounded table prose with exact rendered visible-line-count row height and PDF/render verification.

Workbook cell typography follows a constrained IB hierarchy: Arial 10pt default/body/model cells, 9pt italic gray supporting source/note/unit-helper cells, 10-11pt bold compact section/header cells, and 14pt bold sheet-title cells only. Generated worksheet cells do not use 8pt squeezed text, 10.5pt or other improvised sizes, or 16pt+ presentation headings; crowded renders are fixed through layout, width, copy, or whitespace rather than shrinking text.

evals.name: focused_pricing_module
evals.prompt: 新しいAIプロダクトの価格を決めたい。顧客ROI、原価、粗利、サポート負荷、値上げ余地を踏まえた価格レンジと、投資家に説明できる根拠をExcelで。
evals.expected_output: Lean pricing bundle — exactly Guide, Assumptions, Pricing, Summary (4 tabs, no transitive expansion). Pricing sheet links selected price, customer ROI, value-capture share, cost-to-serve, cost-plus floor, support ratio, and a pass/DD-gate status, plus a row-wise validation plan.

assertions.text: Bundle is exactly Guide, Assumptions, Pricing, Summary — a focused pricing request does not drag in the full 12-sheet workbook

Selected price, customer ROI, value capture, value-based price, cost-to-serve, cost-plus floor, and price support ratio are linked by formulas back to Assumptions inputs (blue) — not restated constants
Price support ratio >= 1x reads as headroom and the pricing gate row flips between pass and DD gate against the stated validation hurdle
Pricing validation plan lists WTP/LOI, pilot conversion, packaging ladder, and renewal-risk tests row-wise with thresholds and owners — without widening period columns
Summary stays compact for the focused bundle: it rolls up only the sheets that exist and the master check covers the registered checks

evals.name: cap_table_jkiss_dilution
evals.prompt: J-KISSで5,000万円調達済み。シリーズAで4億円(ポスト20億円)を調達すると、創業者の持分と議決権はどうなる?SOプール12%も維持したい。資本政策表をExcelで。
evals.expected_output: cap_table mode keeps its 3-sheet state machine (Guide + Kernel + Ownership) with J-KISS conversion at the round, founder issued/FD ownership by round, voting-threshold flags, SO pool range check, and a 100% consistency check.

assertions.text: cap_table mode produces the Guide + Kernel + Ownership state-machine surface, not the full 12-sheet workbook
J-KISS / convertible instruments convert at the priced round under their cap/discount terms and appear in the fully-diluted share count
Founder ownership is shown both issued and fully diluted per round, and the round where founder FD% crosses 66.67% / 50.01% / 33.34% voting thresholds is flagged
SO pool is checked against the 10-15% convention band and the ownership total reconciles to 100% with an explicit check
Pre-money, post-money, price per share, and new shares are internally consistent formulas (post = pre + investment; shares = investment / price)

evals.name: burn_runway_monthly_window
evals.prompt: 手元現金1.2億円、シード2.5億円調達直後。月次でバーンとランウェイを24ヶ月分見たい。いつ資金がショートするかも。
evals.expected_output: burn_runway bundle (Guide, Assumptions, CF, Financing, Summary) on a monthly-first axis anchored to the fiscal year in progress. CF carries a compact operating block (no P&L in the bundle), ending cash, runway months, and a cash-shortfall check; Financing verifies post-raise runway.

assertions.text: Bundle is exactly Guide, Assumptions, CF, Financing, Summary and defaults to a monthly-window axis (hybrid) when no grain is stated — anchored so the first fiscal year is not fully in the past at run time
CF computes revenue, cost, and people lines in a compact in-sheet operating block referencing Assumptions directly — it does not reference a P&L sheet that is not in the bundle
Runway months divides ending cash by the period-normalized burn (using the months ruler), floors at zero burn (cap), and a cash-shortfall check flags any period with negative ending cash
Financing events land in the fiscal year's first monthly column (期首調達) rather than smearing across months, and post-raise runway is checked against the >= 18-month floor
Monthly labels are real calendar months (YYYY/MM) and monthly-window totals reconcile to fiscal-year totals via a bridge check

evals.name: ma_exit_buyer_view
evals.prompt: 事業会社への売却を検討中。買い手目線のバリュエーションレンジ、優先分配後の創業者・投資家の手取り、MOICをExcelで整理して。
evals.expected_output: ma_exit bundle (Guide, Assumptions, Valuation & Exit, IC Memo). Valuation & Exit uses dedicated Low/Mid/High method-matrix columns (never period columns), credibility and use-when per method, a selected EV range, an exit waterfall through preferences, and MOIC that renders '-' when invested is zero.

assertions.text: Bundle is exactly Guide, Assumptions, Valuation & Exit, IC Memo — buyer-view valuation does not require the operating engine sheets
Method matrix rows (revenue multiple, GP multiple, EBITDA multiple, DCF) carry Low/Mid/High in dedicated columns with per-method credibility and use-when guidance, kept separate from any period axis

Exit waterfall walks EV -> net debt -> transaction costs -> preference stack -> common pool -> founder/investor proceeds, and MOIC shows '-' (not a divide-by-zero or MAX(1,x) hack) when invested capital is zero
Downside / Base / Upside exit scenarios are shown and the IC Memo recommendation is gated on the return hurdle and valuation support rather than hardcoded prose
IC Memo covers recommendation, thesis, KPI readout, funding & dilution, valuation & return, what-must-be-true, downside triggers, ranked DD gates, and walk-away conditions

evals.name: market_sizing_isolation
evals.prompt: 国内の建設DX市場でTAM/SAM/SOMを整理したい。うちの計画シェアが過大でないかも確認したい。Excelで。
evals.expected_output: market_sizing bundle (Guide + Evidence). TAM/SAM/SOM live on Evidence as sourced blue inputs with freshness, the final-year share sanity check reads Summary -> Evidence one way only, and missing sources surface as DD-gap rows instead of placeholders.

assertions.text: Bundle is exactly Guide + Evidence — market sizing is an evidence surface, not a revenue driver
TAM/SAM/SOM are blue inputs with source and as-of date; market size never feeds the revenue chain (isolation is one-way: cross-checks reference the market, the engine does not)
Missing or failed external evidence is visible as explicit rows (failed fetch status, 'No external evidence provided — DD gap') — no fabricated SRC-xx placeholders
Market sanity includes a final-year plan-share readout with an interpretation threshold (share above ~30% of SOM needs explicit share-gain evidence)

evals.name: narrative_source_route
evals.prompt: 添付のエクイティストーリー(Markdown)から、資金調達に使える財務モデルをExcelで作って。
evals.expected_output: --source-md narrative route produces the same v2 full 12-sheet bundle (annual grain by default). Stated facts (customer target, monthly price, raise size, gross-margin target) are extracted into the kernel and every generated default is labeled by evidence status.

assertions.text: The narrative route builds the same v2 full bundle as the structured route — 12 sheets, v2 header contract, master check — with the annual grain by default
Stated narrative facts (customer target, monthly price, raise size, target gross margin, stated fiscal years) are extracted and drive the kernel; the demand ramp reaches the stated targets
Every assumption row carries an evidence status (actual / benchmark / management target / estimate / placeholder) and unstated defaults are labeled, not presented as sourced facts
Mechanic detection composes drivers from the narrative (recurring vs marketplace vs asset-heavy) without emitting off-mechanics template rows

evals.expected_output: USD annual full workbook: FY-labeled annual axis, USD scale ladder ($ / $K / $M with accounting formats), generic parameters (no JP-only rows unless stated), and the same v2 contract (freeze, rulers, checks, master check).

The v2 contract holds unchanged in USD: F7 freeze, uniform period width, check rows, master check echo, R17 formula uniformity

evals.name: scenario_toggle_mechanism
evals.prompt: このモデル、シナリオを切り替えたら数字全体が動くようにしてほしい。ダウンサイドで資金がどれだけ追加で要るかも見たい。
evals.expected_output: Scenario mechanism: Assumptions carries a Scenario settings block (Downside/Base/Upside case columns), a blue 1/2/3 toggle, and INDEX-driven effective-scale rows that the engine formulas multiply. Summary shows the 3-case generator snapshot, a downside funding gap, and a snapshot staleness check against the live model.

assertions.text: Assumptions ends with a Scenario settings block: per-driver Downside/Base/Upside scales, a blue scenario toggle (1/2/3), and effective-scale rows computed via INDEX over the case columns
Engine rows (demand, price, variable cost, opex) multiply the effective-scale cells, so flipping the toggle changes computed engine values workbook-wide without editing formulas

Summary shows a Downside/Base/Upside comparison as labeled generator snapshots (final-year revenue/EBITDA, minimum ending cash, additional funding needed) plus a sensitivity matrix snapshot with its assumption note
A snapshot staleness check compares the Base snapshot against the live roll-up while the toggle sits on Base, and registers in the master check
Scenario scales pressure the correct side: cost-labeled drivers raise costs in downside, financing capacity drivers do not inflate revenue

evals.name: evidence_dd_gap_visibility
evals.prompt: 競合の上場企業マルチプルを取ってきて評価に使って。取れなかったらそれも分かるようにして。プライベートの調達事例も2件ある。
evals.expected_output: Evidence register with live public comps (ticker, EV, revenue multiple, as-of, applicability limits), provided private/transaction comps, FAILED fetches visible as failed rows that never pollute medians, and DD-gap rows where evidence is absent.

assertions.text: Live comps appear on Evidence with ticker, source type, as-of date, applicability limits, and status; failed fetches are visible as failed rows and are excluded from applied medians
Provided private / transaction comps from the input are registered alongside public comps with their evidence status
Valid comparable medians flow into the valuation multiples; incomplete comps are registered but not used for medians
Where no external evidence exists the register says so explicitly ('No external evidence provided — DD gap') — the workbook never invents sources
Summary / IC readouts rank the weakest evidence and tie it to next diligence actions

evals.name: workbook_quality_gates
evals.prompt: 生成したモデルが監査に耐えるかチェックして。列幅、フリーズ、単位と表示形式、行内の式の同型性、ハードコード、チェック行まで。
evals.expected_output: Strict audit + quality gates hold on every mode: uniform 11.5 period-column width, F7 freeze anchors, unit-label/number-format agreement, R17 row-formula consistency normalized in R1C1, hardcoded-constant whitelist ({0,1,-1,2,3,12,24,100,365,1000} + sub-1 tolerances), check-row presence with the Summary master check, bundle closure, and zero recalc errors.

assertions.text: Strict audit fails builds with broken sheet references, missing sheet-quality markers, merged cells, wrap_text, manual line breaks, #REF!, or defined names
Period-axis sheets freeze at the first period column row 7 and every period column is exactly 11.5 wide across all sheets

Default-layout label, source/driver, and note columns are content-driven from the widest visible content for that role across the workbook, then applied as one consistent width per role; semantic fill/border spans stay table-local and do not fuse unrelated side-by-side tables.
Unit labels in column E agree with the row's number format (円/千円/百万円/十億円, $/$K/$M, %, x, months, FTE) — money scales must match exactly
Row formulas are copy-identical across period columns after R1C1 normalization, including across the monthly/annual grain boundary
Formulas contain no numeric literals outside the structural whitelist; every economic constant traces to an input cell
LibreOffice recalculation produces zero formula-error cells on representative builds

Model creation is not completion — verify both model logic and sheet design with command-based workbook checks plus rendered-output inspection, fix the concrete failed items, and rerun the same checks until both pass

Public AI-agent or prompt-workflow material is treated as workflow signal only: reusable ideas must become source-bound assumptions, formulas, checks, closeout inspection, or eval/rubric criteria, never financial evidence or fake provenance.

After artifact closeout, user improvement prompts, failed checks, retries, tool/log inefficiency, or cleanup noise, the skill inspects sanitized session evidence, separates one-off artifact fixes from reusable skill gaps, patches the lowest durable layer, adds regression proof, reruns the failed check plus a broader gate, and avoids raw logs, secrets, confidential source text, and source-specific overfitting.

evals.name: self_improvement_from_failed_session_log
evals.prompt: A prior startup-financial-modeling run produced an xlsx, then the user asked for an improvement. Sanitized failed command log: strict audit failed because period formulas diverged after a manual repair; a rerun passed after adding a row-formula consistency check. The run also included an X/public practitioner signal about traces becoming eval cases. Produce the skill-level self-improvement response, not a finance model.
evals.expected_output: A sanitized Reflection Record that classifies the issue as reusable, separates the artifact fix from the skill gap, names the lowest durable layer to patch, proposes a regression proof, strips raw logs/secrets/X handles, states why the lesson is invariant-shaped rather than company-specific, and identifies the broader gate to rerun before closeout.

assertions.text: Output includes a Reflection Record with task type, artifact type, redacted evidence, observed failure, verification evidence, root-cause category, generalized lesson, proposed durable layer, privacy classification, and required regression proof.
The response classifies one-off artifact repair separately from a reusable skill gap and patches the lowest durable layer rather than only adding prose.
The response rejects raw logs, secrets, private source text, X handles, raw public-post quotes, company-specific facts, and numeric incident details from durable skill memory.
The response maps the lesson to a deterministic regression proof and names the failed check plus one broader gate to rerun.
